title: Nancy Fraser and politics [Book Review]
author: Boscov-Ellen

opening_move.description: The review opens with a sweeping diagnosis of contemporary polycrisis (inequality, ecological collapse, displacement, xenophobia), framing Fraser's critical theory as uniquely equipped to trace 'heterogeneous ills' to a common capitalist root.
opening_move.stakes_claimed: We need a critical theory that can illuminate structural connections between apparently disparate crises and point toward emancipatory resolution

theoretical_positioning.description: The review enthusiastically presents Ivković and Zarić's collection as a valuable introduction to and extension of Fraser's political thought, without substantial independent critique.

epistemic_mode.interpretive_methods: critical theory reading of Fraser's work; political-diagnostic framing
epistemic_mode.notes: Book review genre. The polycrisis framing is politically diagnostic but not empirically evidenced; it uses contemporary conditions illustratively.

case_type.case_name: Ivković and Zarić (eds.), Nancy Fraser and Politics (2024); Fraser, Cannibal Capitalism (2022)

confidence_notes: Book review genre; primarily appreciative. The polycrisis framing belongs to the reviewer but is not argued — it functions as a rhetorical stage-setter.
